headline: Hudud Bill Attempts Total 7 Since 1993 with Zero Enactments
context: Kelantan and Terengganu have attempted to enact hudud criminal law 7 times since 1993, each time blocked by federal constitutional requirements for concurrent jurisdiction.

cards.big: PAS has tried to enact hudud law 7 times in 32 years.
cards.sub: Every attempt failed. The constitutional barrier — Article 75 federal supremacy — has not changed.

cards.big: Hudud requires amending the Federal Constitution's Ninth Schedule.
cards.sub: This needs two-thirds parliamentary majority. PAS has never held more than 18% of seats.

cards.big: Private Member's Bill 355 in 2016 sought to raise Shariah court sentencing limits.
cards.sub: The bill did not enact hudud but was positioned as a 'stepping stone.' It was never put to vote.

cards.big: Public support for hudud among Malay-Muslims is 43% in 2025.
cards.sub: Down from 57% in 2015. Support has declined 14 points in a decade.

cards.big: 7 attempts in 32 years with zero enactments. At some point, the exercise is not about passing law — it is about performing loyalty.
The constitutional framework is clear. Hudud requires an amendment that the current political arithmetic cannot deliver. Repeated attempts without the numbers are political theatre, not legislation.
